codeReview.summary: Implementation fully satisfies the feature requirements. Version history tracking via memory_versions table, rollback capability via rollback_to_version(), and audit trail via rollback_audit_log table are all implemented correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed established Rust conventions in AGENTS.md: async_trait patterns, proper error handling with SwellError, structured logging via tracing, and tokio::test for async tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker correctly used SwellError::DatabaseError for SQL errors, proper ? propagation, and tested with in-memory SQLite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the Rust skill guidance, successfully avoiding ownership pitfalls. No borrow checker battles in the implementation - all UUIDs and strings handled correctly with proper cloning where needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker used .clone() appropriately (e.g., current.content.clone()) and avoided moving values after use.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used only standard build/test commands from services.yaml. No external services required for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands: cargo build --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace, cargo test -p swell-memory

summary: Feature 'mem-version-rollback' passes scrutiny review. Implementation correctly provides: (1) Version history per memory via memory_versions table with proper indexing; (2) Rollback to specific version via rollback_to_version() which creates a pre-rollback snapshot before restoring target version content/metadata; (3) Audit trail of rollbacks via rollback_audit_log table. All 10 unit tests pass. Build and clippy pass without warnings.
